message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>This study examines potential, subtle and persistent adverse effects of COVID-19 vaccines on the cardiovascular system. Vaccine-associated myocardial injury was analysed by measuring high-sensitive troponin T (hsTnT); mid-regional pro-adrenomedullin (MR-proADM) levels were evaluated to assess endothelial dysfunction.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    This was a prospective study with a vulnerable population of healthcare workers (HCWs) and elderly patients (> 70 years) who were vaccinated with either one dose of ChAdOx1 nCov-19 adenoviral vector vaccine (AZ) followed by one dose of the BNT162b2 messenger RNA vaccine (BNT), or with two doses of BNT (12
                    <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                    of January - 30
                    <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                    of November 2021). HsTnT and MR-proADM were measured in blood samples at three visits (V
                    <jats:sub>1</jats:sub>
                    : 1
                    <jats:sup>st</jats:sup>
                    immediately before vaccination; V
                    <jats:sub>2, 3</jats:sub>
                    : 3-4 weeks after 1
                    <jats:sup>st</jats:sup>
                    and 2
                    <jats:sup>nd</jats:sup>
                    vaccination). HsTnT of HCWs was compared to a healthy reference population.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    N=162 volunteers were included (V
                    <jats:sub>1</jats:sub>
                    =161; V
                    <jats:sub>2</jats:sub>
                    , V
                    <jats:sub>3</jats:sub>
                    =162 each). N=74 (45.7%) received AZ/BNT and n=88 (54.3%) received BNT/BNT (elderly: n=20 (12.3%), HCWs: n=68 (42.0%)). Median hsTnT levels were 4ng/L, 5ng/L and 4ng/L (V
                    <jats:sub>1</jats:sub>
                    -V
                    <jats:sub>3</jats:sub>
                    ) for AZ/BNT and at 5ng/L, 6ng/L and 6ng/L (V
                    <jats:sub>1</jats:sub>
                    -V
                    <jats:sub>3</jats:sub>
                    ) for BNT/BNT. Compared to the reference population (n=300), hsTnT was significantly higher at all visits for both vaccination groups (p<0.01), without differences between the AZ/BNT and BNT/BNT cohort. MR-proADM values were 0.43nmol/L, 0.45nmol/L, 0.44nmol/L (V
                    <jats:sub>1</jats:sub>
                    -V
                    <jats:sub>3</jats:sub>
                    ) in the AZ/BNT cohort and 0.49nmol/L, 0.44nmol/L, 0.47nmol/L for BNT/BNT, respectively. Change of median hsTnT and MR-proADM between visits did not show significant increases. One HCW case had a permanent and three a transient hsTnT increase ≥14ng/L.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    With one individual exception, no overall subtle, persistent cardiovascular involvement was observed after the 2
                    <jats:sup>nd</jats:sup>
                    COVID-19 vaccination.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Structured graphical abstract</jats:title>
                  <jats:fig id="ufig1" position="float" orientation="portrait" fig-type="figure">
                    <jats:caption>
                      <jats:p>
                        Summary of the vaccination scheme and visiting points in the study population (of HCWs and seniors > 70 years) between the 12
                        <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                        of January and the 30
                        <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                        of November 2021. The results showed no overall subtle, chronic myocardial or vascular involvement in our COVID-19 vaccinated cohorts.
                      </jats:p>
                      <jats:p>
                        <jats:underline>Abbreviations:</jats:underline>
                        AZ ChAdOx1 nCov-19 adenoviral vector vaccine from Astra Zeneca, BNT BNT162b2 messenger ribonucleic acid vaccine from BioNTech, EDTA Ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, HCWs health care workers, hsTnT high-sensitive troponin T, mid-regional pro-adrenomedullin, V
                        <jats:sub>1</jats:sub>
                        -V
                        <jats:sub>3</jats:sub>
                        visiting times 1-3, w week(s).
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:caption>
                    <jats:graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="24307207v1_ufig1" position="float" orientation="portrait"/>
                  </jats:fig>
                </jats:sec>
title: Levels of high-sensitive troponin T and mid-regional pro-adrenomedullin after COVID-19 vaccination in vulnerable groups: a prospective study on subtle and persistent cardiovascular involvement

reference.article-title: The effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines in reducing the incidence, hospitalization, and mortality from COVID-19: A systematic review and meta-analysis
